title: Round Trip Invariant Failures
findings: 发现 2 个 round-trip invariant 问题：project-scoped route-write 写入后不会被 archive/review 写路径实际使用；fab_recall include_related 的 bare related-id 契约与 store-qualified 候选 id 不匹配。已避开现有 switch-write snapshot、doctor 集成、G11 stale 等重复 issue。
issues_found: [{"title":"Project route-write is ignored by archive/review write-target resolution","severity":"high","description":"Invariant: after `fabric store route-write project:fabric-v2 platform`, a team-layer archive in a repo bound to `active_project=fabric-v2` should land in the routed `platform` store and record matching visibility. The shared resolver supports project routes (`packages/shared/test/store/privacy-boundary.test.ts:55`), and the CLI persists routes (`packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts:476`), but server write-target helpers always resolve the literal `team` scope for team-layer writes (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60`, `packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:115`) before later stamping `semantic_scope: project:<active_project>` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:123`). `extractKnowledge` and review promotion/layer-flip use those helpers (`packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:359`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:607`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:964`), so the config write round-trip silently falls back to the default/active team store instead of the route.","location":"packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60","fix_direction":"Derive an effective write scope once (`personal` for personal, otherwise `project:${active_project}` when present, else `team`) and pass that scope into `resolveWriteTarget` for both target dir and visibility metadata. Add an integration test that seeds two writable stores, sets `write_routes: [{scope:'project:fabric-v2', store:'platform'}]` plus `active_project:'fabric-v2'`, runs `extractKnowledge` and review approve, then asserts pending/canonical paths, counters, and `visibility_store` land in `platform`.","affected_components":["cross-store write","fab_extract_knowledge","fab_review approve/modify-layer","store route-write","StoreResolver"],"tags":["round-trip-invariants","route-write","archive-review","store-routing"]},{"title":"fab_recall include_related only works with store-qualified related IDs despite bare-id contract","severity":"medium","description":"Invariant: a frontmatter edge authored as `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` should round-trip through plan candidates and `fab_recall({include_related:true})` to fetch that neighbor. The schema/builder document and parse bare stable ids (`packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts:80`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1070`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1245`), while cross-store recall emits store-qualified candidate ids via `entry.qualifiedId` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts:163`). `recall` compares `candidate.description.related` directly against store-qualified `candidateIds` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:137`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:148`), and the test only passes because it seeds a non-contract `related: [team:KT-GLD-0001]` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts:196`). Real bare related edges therefore neither expand nor produce the next-step nudge.","location":"packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132","fix_direction":"Normalize related ids before comparison: accept bare local ids by qualifying them with the source candidate's store alias when possible, or compare against both local and qualified id maps. Update recall tests to seed `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` and add cross-store ambiguity coverage so include_related behavior is deterministic.","affected_components":["fab_recall","include_related","knowledge graph related edges","cross-store recall","agents-meta frontmatter parsing"],"tags":["round-trip-invariants","recall","related-edges","store-qualified-id"]}]
